questions.question: The cost of a washing machine is 25% less than the cost of a TV. If the cost of the washing machine increases by 30% and that of the TV decreases by 19%, then what is the percentage change in the total cost of 8 washing machines and 4 TVs?<\/p>\r\n
A dealer sells two dryers at the rate of \u20b972,000 per dryer. On one, he earns a profit of 8% and on the other he loses 25%. What is his loss percentage in the whole transaction?<\/p>\r\n

In quadrilateral PQRS, the bisectors of ∠R and ∠S meet at the point T (inside the quadrilateral) and ∠STR = 27.5°. If the ratio of ∠P to ∠Q is 4 : 7, then what is the difference between the measures of ∠Q and ∠P?<\/p>\r\n

A dealer buys two articles X and Y for \u20b91,500 each. He marks each of them at the same price. He sells X by giving two successive discounts of 25% and 44% and still earns \u20b9279 as profit. If he sells Y at a single discount of 58%, then what is the profit percentage on Y?<\/p>\r\n

In triangle ABC, D,E, and F are the midpoints of sides BC, CA, and AB, respectively. The medians AD, BE, and CF meet at point G. What is the ratio of the length of the median to the distance from the vertex to the centroid?<\/p>\r\n

Creating a frustum, a right circular cone is sliced parallel to its base,. The height of the original cone is 35 cm , and the cut is made 14 cm from the vertex. What is the ratio of the volumes of the frustum to that of the entire cone?<\/p>\r\n

Three pipes P, Q, and R can fill a tank in 38, 44, and 28 minutes respectively. Initially, the tank is empty. Pipe P is opened at 9:41 AM, and pipe Q is opened 8 minutes later. How many minutes after opening pipe Q should pipe R be opened so that the tank is exactly full by 10:00 AM?<\/p>\r\n

Ananya runs a clothing store and has a marked price of \u20b912,000 for a winter coat. She offers two successive discounts: 12% and 18%. Ananya wants to know what single equivalent discount would result in the same final selling price as the two successive discounts. What is the single equivalent discount Ananya should offer to achieve the same final price as the two successive discounts?<\/p>\r\n
If sin(\u20613x - 10)°= cos(\u2061x + 20)°, then x= <\/p>\r\n

A sum of \u20b910,00,000 is invested in a bank at 11% interest per annum compounded annually. On maturity, the amount payable becomes \u20b913,67,631. Find the time period of the investment.<\/p>\r\n
Two trains of lengths 316 m and 349 m are running at speeds of 58 km\/h and 68 km\/h, respectively, on parallel tracks in opposite directions. In how many seconds will they cross each other?<\/p>\r\n

An antique dealer sold three items, A, B and C, with their selling prices in the ratio 4:5:6. He suffered a loss of 10% on item A, made a profit of 50% on item B, and broke even (no profit, no loss) on item C. What was his approximate percentage gain or loss in the overall deal?<\/p>\r\n
The average of 7 numbers is 23. If each number is increased by 4, what will the new average be?<\/p>\r\n

A shopkeeper sells an item for \u20b9324.3 after giving two successive discounts of 60% and 31% on its marked price. Had he not given any discount, he would have earned a profit of 25%. What is the cost price (in \u20b9) of the item?<\/p>\r\n
The circumference of a circle is 132 cm. The radius of the given circle is equal to the radius of a cone having height 12 cm. Find the volume of the cone. (Take π = 22\/7)<\/p>\r\n
A boat goes 413 km upstream in 59 hours and a distance of 621 km downstream in 69 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water. (In kmph)<\/p>\r\n
The curved surface area and the volume of a cylindrical pipe is 565.2 m2<\/sup> and 1,695.6 m3,<\/sup> respectively. Find the total surface area of the pipe. (Use π = 3.14)<\/p>\r\n

The area of a quadrilateral ABCD is 299 cm2<\/sup>. The lengths of the perpendiculars from the vertices B and D to the diagonal AC are 11 cm and 12 cm, respectively. Determine the length (in cm) of the diagonal AC.<\/p>\r\n
X can finish a job in 147 days. He worked for 63 days alone and the remaining work is completed by Y, in 84 days. How many days would both together complete the work?<\/p>\r\n

Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: Introduction sentence:</strong>
Health insurance has become an essential component of financial planning in India, particularly in light of rising medical costs and unpredictable health emergencies.
P.</strong> consequently, individuals should carefully examine policy coverage, exclusions, waiting periods, and claim settlement records before finalising an insurance plan.
Q.</strong> for example, opting for a policy solely on the basis of low premium may result in inadequate coverage during critical medical situations.
R</strong>. nevertheless, many policyholders fail to read the fine print and assume that all medical expenses will be covered without limitations.
S</strong>. such assumptions often lead to dissatisfaction and financial stress at the time of hospitalisation.
 </p>

Sentence Improvement</strong>
She was ingenuous</u> enough to accept the flattering remarks  without suspicion, but she was also ingenious</u> enough to optimise the workflow and reduce delays in the project.
 </p>

S1 : The definition of large farmers differs  from state to state in India.
S6: This trend increased the income base of those who were already relatively well-off and better placed in society. 
P : In the initial phase of the Green Revolution, the large farmers were able to adopt the High Yielding Varieties easily.
Q : The new varieties increased their savings, both to buy machinery that can displace labour and to purchase more land.
R : For example, a farmer having 10 acres  in Kerala is a large farmer, while in  Rajasthan, Punjab and Haryana he falls  under the category of a small or medium farmer.
S : All the studies conducted in areas where Green Revolution is a success show that the large farmers have been the main gainers of the package programme.
 </p>

questions.question:
Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
P. </strong>The authorities responded harshly to the movement by imposing severe restrictions on the workers and attempting to crush their resolve through intimidation and deprivation.
Q. </strong>Gandhiji, upon assuming leadership of the struggle, carefully planned a peaceful yet determined march to challenge the unjust laws imposed on the labourers.
R.</strong> Despite repeated arrests and continuous harassment by the administration, the march continued with unwavering discipline and moral courage.
S. </strong>The workers, inspired by Gandhiji’s presence and guidance, remained united and resolute, even when faced with imprisonment and physical hardship.
T.</strong> Eventually, many of them were jailed, subjected to inhuman treatment, and forced into hard labour, yet their spirit of resistance did not weaken.</p>
